MAT_3_15: But Jesus answered and said to him, Permit now, for this is necessary for us so that all righteousness may be fulfilled; and then he permitted him.
MAT_4_4: But he answered and said, It is written, that it is not by bread alone that man can live, but by every word which comes from the mouth of God.
MAT_4_7: Jesus said to him, Again it is written, that you shall not try out the Lord your God.
MAT_4_10: Then Jesus said to him, Go away, Satan, for it is written, You shall worship the Lord your God, and him only shall you serve.
MAT_4_17: From that time Jesus began to preach and to say, Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is coming near.
MAT_4_19: And Jesus said to them, Come after me, and I will make you to become fishers of men.
MAT_5_3: Blessed are the poor in pride, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
MAT_5_4: Blessed are they who mourn, for they shall be comforted.
MAT_5_5: Blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth.
MAT_5_6: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for justice, for they shall be well satisfied.
MAT_5_7: Blessed are the merciful, for to them shall be mercy.
MAT_5_8: Blessed are those who are pure in their hearts, for they shall see God.
MAT_5_9: Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called sons of God.
MAT_5_10: Blessed are those who are persecuted for the sake of justice, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
MAT_5_11: Blessed are you, when they reproach you and persecute you, and speak against you every kind of bad word, falsely, for my sake,
MAT_5_12: Then be glad and rejoice, for your reward is increased in heaven; for in this very manner they persecuted the prophets who were before you.
MAT_5_13: You are indeed the salt of the earth; but if the salt should lose its savor, with what could it be salted? It would not be worth anything, but to be thrown outside and to be trodden down by men.
MAT_5_14: You are indeed the light of the world; a city that is built upon a mountain cannot be hidden.
MAT_5_15: Nor do they light a lamp and put it under a basket, but on a lamp holder, so it gives light to all who are in the house.
MAT_5_16: Let your light thus shine before men, so that they may see your good works and glorify your Father in heaven.
MAT_5_17: Do not expect that I have come to weaken the law or the prophets; I have not come to weaken, but to fulfil.
MAT_5_18: For truly I tell you, Until heaven and earth pass away, not even one yoth * or a dash shall pass away from the law until all of it is fulfilled.
MAT_5_19: Whoever therefore tries to weaken even one of these smallest commandments, and teaches men so, he shall be regarded as small in the kingdom of heaven; but anyone who observes and teaches them, he shall be regarded as great in the kingdom of heaven.
MAT_5_20: For I say to you, that unless your righteousness exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees, you shall not enter the kingdom of heaven.
MAT_5_21: You have heard that it was said to those who were before you, You shall not kill, and whoever kills is guilty before the court.
MAT_5_22: But I say to you, that whoever becomes angry with his brother for no reason, is guilty before the court: and whoever should say to his brother, Raca (which means, I spit on you) is guilty before the congregation; and whoever says to his brother, you are a nurse maid, is condemned to hell fire.
MAT_5_23: If it should happen therefore that while you are presenting your offering upon the altar, and right there you remember that your brother has any grievance against you,
MAT_5_24: Leave your offering there upon the altar, and first go and make peace with your brother, and then come back and present your offering.
MAT_5_25: Try to get reconciled with your accuser promptly, while you are going on the road with him; for your accuser might surrender you to the judge, and the judge would commit you to the jailer, and you will be cast into prison.
MAT_5_26: Truly I say to you, that you would never come out thence until you had paid the last cent.
MAT_5_27: You have heard that it is said, You shall not commit adultery.
MAT_5_28: But I say to you, that whoever looks at a woman with the desire to covet her, has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
MAT_5_29: If your right eye should cause you to stumble, pluck it out and throw it away from you; for it is better for you to lose one of your members, and not all your body fall into hell.
MAT_5_30: And if your right hand should cause you to stumble, cut it off and throw it away from you; for it is better for you to lose one of your members, and not all your body fall into hell.
MAT_5_31: It has been said that whoever divorces his wife, must give her the divorce papers.
MAT_5_32: But I say to you, that whoever divorces his wife, except for fornication, causes her to commit adultery; and whoever marries a woman who is separated but not divorced, commits adultery.
MAT_5_33: Again you have heard it was said to them who were before you, that you shall not lie in your oaths, but entrust your oaths to the Lord.
MAT_5_34: But I say to you, never swear; neither by heaven, because it is God’s throne;
MAT_5_35: Nor by the earth, for it is a stool under his feet; nor by Jerusalem, for it is the city of a great king.
MAT_5_36: Neither shall you swear by your own head, because you cannot create in it a single black or white hair.
MAT_5_37: But let your words be yes, yes, and no, no; for anything which adds to these is a deception.
MAT_5_38: You have heard that it is said, An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth.
MAT_5_39: But I say to you, that you should not resist evil; but whoever strikes you on your right cheek, turn to him the other also.
MAT_5_40: And if anyone wishes to sue you at the court and take away your shirt, let him have your robe also.
MAT_5_41: Whoever compels you to carry a burden for a mile, go with him two.
MAT_5_42: Whoever asks from you, give him; and whoever wishes to borrow from you, do not refuse him.
MAT_5_43: You have heard that it is said, Be kind to your friend, and hate your enemy.
MAT_5_44: But I say to you, Love your enemies, and bless anyone who curses you, and do good to anyone who hates you, and pray for them who carry you away by force and persecute you,
MAT_5_45: So that you may become sons of your Father who is in heaven, who causes his sun to shine upon the good and upon the bad, and who pours down his rain upon the just and upon the unjust.
MAT_5_46: For if you love only those who love you, what reward will you have? Do not even the publicans do the same thing?
MAT_5_47: And if you salute only your brothers, what is it more that you do? Do not even the publicans do the same thing?
MAT_5_48: Therefore, you become perfect, just as your Father in heaven is perfect.
MAT_6_1: BE careful concerning your alms, not to do them in the presence of men, merely that they may see them; otherwise you have no reward with your Father in heaven.
MAT_6_2: Therefore when you give alms, do not blow a trumpet before you, just as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the market places, so that they may be glorified by men. Truly I say to you, that they have already received their reward.
MAT_6_3: But when you give alms, let not your left hand know what your right hand is doing;
MAT_6_4: So that your alms may be done secretly, and your Father who sees in secret, shall himself reward you openly.
MAT_6_5: And when you pray, do not be like the hypocrites, who like to pray, standing in the synagogues and at the street corners, so that they may be seen by men. Truly I say to you, that they have already received their reward.
MAT_6_6: But you, when you pray, enter into your inner chamber, and lock your door, and pray to your Father who is in secret, and your Father who sees in secret he himself shall reward you openly.
MAT_6_7: And when you pray, do not repeat your words like the pagans, for they think that because of much talking they will be heard.
MAT_6_8: Therefore, do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need, before you ask him.
MAT_6_9: Therefore pray in this manner: Our Father in heaven, hallowed be thy name.
MAT_6_10: Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done, as in heaven so on earth.
MAT_6_11: Give us bread for our needs from day to day.
MAT_6_12: And forgive us our offences, as we have forgiven our offenders;
MAT_6_13: And do not let us enter into temptation, but deliver us from error. Because thine is the kingdom and the power and the glory for ever and ever. Amen.
MAT_6_14: For if you forgive men their faults, your Father in heaven will also forgive you.
MAT_6_15: But if you do not forgive men, neither will your Father forgive even your faults.
MAT_6_16: When you fast, do not look sad like the hypocrites; for they disfigure their faces, so that they may appear to men that they are fasting. Truly I say to you, that they have already received their reward.
MAT_6_17: But you, when you fast, wash your face and anoint your head;
MAT_6_18: So that it may not appear to men that you are fasting, but to your Father who is in secret; and your Father who sees in secret, he will reward you.
MAT_6_19: Do not lay up for yourselves treasures buried in the ground, a place where rust and moth destroy, and where thieves break through and steal.
MAT_6_20: But lay up for yourselves a treasure in heaven, where neither rust nor moth destroy, and where thieves do not break through and steal.
MAT_6_21: For where your treasure is, there also is your heart.
MAT_6_22: The eye is the lamp of the body; if therefore your eye be clear, your whole body is also lighted.
MAT_6_23: But if your eye is diseased, your whole body will be dark. If therefore the light that is in you is darkness, how much more will be your darkness.
MAT_6_24: No man can serve two masters; for either he will hate the one, and like the other; or he will honor one, and despise the other. You cannot serve God and mammon (wealth).
MAT_6_25: For this reason, I say to you, Do not worry for your life, what you will eat, and what you will drink, nor for your body, what you will wear. Behold, is not life much more important than food, and the body than clothing?
MAT_6_26: Watch the birds of the sky, for they do not sow, neither do they harvest, nor gather into barns, and yet your Father in heaven feeds them. Are you not much more important than they?
MAT_6_27: Who is among you who by worrying can add one cubit to his stature?
MAT_6_28: Why do you worry about clothing? Observe the wild flowers, how they grow; they do not get tired out, nor do they spin.
MAT_6_29: But I say to you, that not even Solomon with all of his glory was covered like one of them.
MAT_6_30: Now if God clothes in such fashion the grass of the field, which today is and tomorrow falls into the fireplace, is he not much more to you, O you of little faith?
MAT_6_31: Therefore do not worry or say, What will we eat, or what will we drink, or with what will we be clothed?
MAT_6_32: For worldly people seek after all these things. Your Father in heaven knows that all of these things are also necessary for you.
MAT_6_33: But you seek first the kingdom of God and his righteousness, and all of these things shall be added to you.
MAT_6_34: Therefore do not worry for tomorrow; for tomorrow will look after its own. Sufficient for each day, is its own trouble.
MAT_7_1: JUDGE not, that you may not be judged.
MAT_7_2: For with the same judgment that you judge, you will be judged, and with the same measure with which you measure, it will be measured to you.
MAT_7_3: Why do you see the splinter which is in your brother’s eye, and do not feel the beam which is in your own eye?
MAT_7_4: Or how can you say to your brother, let me take out the splinter from your eye, and behold there is a cross beam in your own eye?
MAT_7_5: O hypocrites, first take out the beam from your own eye, and then you will see clearly to get out the splinter from your brother’s eye.
MAT_7_6: Do not give holy things to the dogs; and do not throw your pearls before the swine, for they might tread them with their feet, and then turn and rend you.
MAT_7_7: Ask, and it shall be given to you; seek, and you shall find; knock and it shall be opened to you.
MAT_7_8: For whoever asks, receives; and he who seeks, finds; and to him who knocks, the door is opened.
MAT_7_9: Or who is the man among you, who when his son asks him for bread, why, will he hand him a stone?
MAT_7_10: Or if he should ask him for fish, why, will he hand him a snake?
MAT_7_11: If therefore you who err, know how to give good gifts to your sons, how much more will your Father in heaven give good things to those who ask him?
MAT_7_12: Whatever you wish men to do for you, do likewise also for them; for this is the law and the prophets.
MAT_7_13: Enter in through the narrow door, for wide is the door, and broad is the road which carries to destruction, and many are those who travel on it.
MAT_7_14: O how narrow is the door, and how difficult is the road which carries to life, and few are those who are found on it.
MAT_7_15: Be careful of false prophets who come to you in lamb’s clothing, but within they are ravening wolves.
MAT_7_16: You will know them by their fruits. Why, do they gather grapes from thorns, or figs from thistles?
MAT_7_17: So every good tree bears good fruits; but a bad tree bears bad fruits.
MAT_7_18: A good tree cannot bear bad fruits, neither can a bad tree bear good fruits.
MAT_7_19: Any tree which does not bear good fruits will be cut down and cast into the fire.
MAT_7_20: Thus by their fruits you will know them.
MAT_7_21: It is not everyone who merely says to me, My Lord, my Lord, will enter into the kingdom of heaven, but he who does the will of my Father in heaven.
MAT_7_22: A great many will say to me in that day, My Lord, my Lord, did we not prophesy in your name, and in your name cast out devils, and in your name do many wonders?
MAT_7_23: Then I will declare to them, I have never known you; keep away from me, O you that work iniquity.
MAT_7_24: Therefore whoever hears these words of mine, and does them, he is like a wise man, who built his house upon a rock.
MAT_7_25: And the rain came down, and the rivers overflowed, and the winds blew, and they beat upon that house; but it did not fall down, because its foundations were laid upon a rock.
MAT_7_26: And whoever hears these words of mine, and does them not, is like a foolish man, who built his house upon sand.
MAT_7_27: And the rain came down, and the rivers overflowed, and the winds blew, and they beat upon that house; and it fell down, and its fall was very great.

MAT_8_4: Jesus then said to him, Look here, why are you telling it to men? go first and show yourself to the priests, and offer an offering as Moses has commanded, for a testimonial to them.
MAT_8_7: Jesus said to him, I will come and heal him.
MAT_8_10: When Jesus heard it, he was amazed, and he said to those who accompanied him, Truly I say to you, that not even in Israel have I found such faith as this.
MAT_8_11: And I say to you, that a great many will come from the east and from the west, and sit down with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.
MAT_8_12: But the sons of the kingdom will be put out in the outer darkness; there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
MAT_8_13: So Jesus said to the centurion, Go, let it be done to you according to your belief. And his boy was healed in that very hour.
MAT_8_20: Jesus said to him, The foxes have holes, and the fowls of the sky a sheltering place, but the Son of man has no place even to lay his head.
MAT_8_22: But Jesus said to him, Come after me, and let the dead bury their own dead.
MAT_8_26: Jesus said to them, Why are you fearful, O you of little faith? Then he got up and rebuked the wind and the sea, and there was a great calm.
MAT_9_2: And they brought to him a paralytic, lying on a quilt-bed; and Jesus saw their faith, and he said to the paralytic, Have courage, my son; your sins have been forgiven.
MAT_9_4: But Jesus knew their thoughts; so he said to them, Why do you think evil in your hearts?
MAT_9_5: For which is easier to say, Your sins have been forgiven, or to say, Arise and walk?
MAT_9_6: But that you might know that the Son of man has authority on earth to forgive sins, then he said to the paralytic, Arise, take up your quilt-bed, and go to your home.
MAT_9_9: And as Jesus passed from that place, he saw a man whose name was Matthew, sitting in the custom house, and he said to him, Follow me; and he got up and went after him.
MAT_9_12: But when Jesus heard it, he said to them, Those who are well need no doctor, but those who are seriously sick.
MAT_9_13: Go and learn what this means, I want mercy and not sacrifice; for I came not to invite righteous men, but sinners.
MAT_9_15: Jesus said to them, Is it possible for those at the wedding feast to fast as long as the bridegroom is with them? But the days are coming, when the bridegroom will be taken from them, and then they will fast.
MAT_9_16: No man puts a new patch on an old garment, so as not to weaken that garment, and make the hole larger.
MAT_9_17: Neither do they pour new wine into worn out skins, so as not to rend the skins, and spill the wine, and the wine runs out, and the skins are ruined; but they pour new wine into new skins, and both of them are well preserved.
MAT_9_22: And Jesus turned around and saw her and said to her, Have courage, my daughter, your faith has healed you; and the woman was healed in that very hour.
MAT_9_24: And he said to them, That is enough; for the little girl is not dead, but she is asleep; and they laughed at him.
MAT_9_28: And when he came into the house, the same blind men came up to him. Jesus said to them, Do you believe that I can do this? They said to him, Yes, our Lord.
MAT_9_29: Then he touched their eyes and said, Let it be to you according to your faith.
MAT_9_30: And immediately their eyes were opened; and Jesus charged them and said, See that no one knows it.
MAT_9_37: So he said to his disciples, The harvest is great, and the laborers are few;
MAT_9_38: Therefore urge the owner of the harvest to bring more laborers to his harvest.
MAT_10_5: These twelve Jesus sent out, and charged them and said, Keep away from pagan practices, and do not enter a Samaritan city;
MAT_10_6: But above all, go to the sheep which are lost from the house of Israel.
MAT_10_7: And as you go, preach and say, that the kingdom of heaven is near.
MAT_10_8: Heal the sick, cleanse the lepers, cast out demons; freely you have received, freely give.
MAT_10_9: Do not accumulate gold, nor silver, nor brass in your purses;
MAT_10_10: Nor a bag for the journey, nor two shirts and shoes, nor a staff; for a laborer is at least worthy of his food.
MAT_10_11: Whatever city or town you enter, ask who is trustworthy in it, and remain there until you leave.
MAT_10_12: And when you enter into the house, salute the family.
MAT_10_13: And if the family is trustworthy, your salutation of peace shall come upon it; but if it is not trustworthy, your salutation shall return to you.
MAT_10_14: Whoever will not welcome you, and will not listen to your words, when you leave the house or the village, shake off the sand from your feet.
MAT_10_15: Truly I say to you, that it will be easier for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah on the day of judgment than for that city.
MAT_10_16: Behold, I am sending you like lambs among the wolves; therefore be wise as serpents, and pure as doves.
MAT_10_17: But be careful of men; for they will deliver you up to the courts, and they will scourge you in their synagogues;
MAT_10_18: And they will bring you before the presence of governors and kings for my sake, as a testimony to them and to the Gentiles.
MAT_10_19: But when they deliver you up, do not worry as to how or what you will speak; for it will be given to you in that very hour what you are to speak.
MAT_10_20: For it is not you who speak, but the Spirit of your Father, which speaks through you.
MAT_10_21: Brother will deliver up his own brother to death, and father his son; and children will rise up against their parents and put them to death.
MAT_10_22: And you will be hated by everybody because of my name; but he who endures until the end shall live.
MAT_10_23: When they persecute you in this city, escape to another; for truly I say to you, that you shall not finish converting all the cities of the house of Israel, until the Son of man returns.
MAT_10_24: No disciple is more important than his teacher, and no servant than his master.
MAT_10_25: It is enough for a disciple to be like his teacher, and for a servant to be like his master. If then, they have called the master of the house Beelzebub, how much more those of his household.
MAT_10_26: Therefore do not be afraid of them; for there is nothing covered that will not be uncovered, and hidden that will not be known.
MAT_10_27: What I tell you in the dark, tell it in the daylight; and what you hear with your ears, preach on the house tops.
MAT_10_28: Do not be afraid of those who kill the body, but who cannot kill the soul; but above all, be afraid of him who can destroy both the soul and the body in hell.
MAT_10_29: Are not two sparrows sold for a penny? And yet not one of them will fall on the ground without your Father’s will.
MAT_10_30: But so far as you are concerned, even the hairs of your head are all numbered.
MAT_10_31: Therefore fear not; you are much more important than many sparrows.
MAT_10_32: Everyone therefore who will acknowledge me before men, I will also acknowledge him before my Father in heaven.
MAT_10_33: But whoever will deny me before men, I will also deny him before my Father in heaven.
MAT_10_34: Do not expect that I have come to bring peace on earth; I have not come to bring peace but a sword.
MAT_10_35: For I have come to set a man against his father, and a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.
MAT_10_36: And a man’s enemies will be the members of his own household.
MAT_10_37: Whoever loves father or mother more than me is not worthy of me; and whoever loves son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me.
MAT_10_38: And whoever does not take up his cross and follow me is not worthy of me.
MAT_10_39: He who is concerned about his life shall lose it; and he who loses his life for my sake shall find it.
MAT_10_40: Whoever receives you, receives me; and whoever receives me, receives him who sent me.
MAT_10_41: He who receives a prophet in the name of a prophet, shall receive a prophet’s reward; and whoever receives a righteous man in the name of a righteous man, shall receive a righteous man’s reward.
MAT_10_42: Anyone who gives a drink to one of these little ones, if only a cup of cold water, in the name of a disciple, truly I say to you, he shall never lose his reward.
MAT_11_4: Jesus answered and said, Go and describe to John the things which you see and hear.
MAT_11_5: The blind see, and the lame walk, and the lepers are cleansed, and the deaf hear, and the dead rise up, and the poor are given hope.
MAT_11_6: And blessed is he who does not stumble on account of me.
MAT_11_7: When they went away, Jesus began to speak to the people concerning John, What did you go out to the wilderness to see? A reed which is shaken by the wind?
MAT_11_8: If not so, what did you go out to see? A man dressed in fine clothes? Behold those who wear fine clothes are in kings’ houses.
MAT_11_9: And if not so, What then did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and much more than a prophet.
MAT_11_10: For this is he of whom it is written, Behold, I send my messenger before your face, to prepare the way before you.
MAT_11_11: Truly I say to you, that among those who are born of women, there has never risen one who is greater than John the Baptist; and yet even the least person in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
MAT_11_12: From the days of John the Baptist until now, the kingdom of heaven has been administered by force, and only those in power control it.
MAT_11_13: For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John.
MAT_11_14: And if you wish, accept it, that he is Elijah who was to come.
MAT_11_15: He who has ears to hear, let him hear.
MAT_11_16: But to whom shall I liken this generation? It is like boys who sit in the street and call to their friends.
MAT_11_17: And say, We have sung to you, but you would not dance; and we have wailed to you but you did not mourn.
MAT_11_18: For John came, neither eating nor drinking, and they said he is crazy.
MAT_11_19: The Son of man came, eating and drinking, and they said, Behold, a glutton and a wine- bibber, and a friend of publicans and sinners. And yet wisdom is justified by its works.
MAT_11_21: Woe to you, Chorazin! woe to you, Bethsaida! for if in Tyre and Sidon had been done the works which were done in you, they might have repented in sackcloth and ashes.
MAT_11_22: But I say to you, It will be easier for Tyre and Sidon in the day of judgment, than for you.
MAT_11_23: And you, Capernaum, which have exalted yourself up to heaven, shall be brought down to Sheol; for if in Sodom had been done the works which were done in you, it would be standing to this day.
MAT_11_24: But I say to you, It will be easier for the land of Sodom in the judgment day, than for you.
MAT_11_25: At that time, Jesus answered and said, I thank you, O my Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and the men of understanding, and you have revealed them to children.
MAT_11_26: O yes, my Father, for such was your will.
MAT_11_27: Everything has been delivered to me by my Father, and no man knows the Son except the Father, nor does any man know the Father but the Son, and he to whomever the Son wishes to reveal.
MAT_11_28: Come to me, all you who are tired out and carrying burdens, and I will give you rest.
MAT_11_29: Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me, for I am genial and meek in my heart, and you will find rest to your souls.
MAT_11_30: For my yoke is very pleasant, and my burden is light.

MAT_12_5: Or, have you not read in the book of law, that the priests in the temple disregard the sabbath, and yet are blameless?
MAT_12_6: But I say to you, that there is one here, who is greater than the temple.

MAT_12_26: And if Satan cast out Satan. he is divided against himself; how then will his kingdom stand?

MAT_12_28: And if I cast out devils by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God has come near to you.

MAT_12_30: He who is not with me is against me; and he who does not gather with me, shall be dispersed.

MAT_12_34: O generation of scorpions, how can you speak good things when you are bad? For the mouth speaks from the fullness of the heart.
MAT_12_35: A good man brings out good things from good treasures, and a bad man brings out bad things from bad treasures.

MAT_21_43: Therefore I say to you, that the kingdom of God will be taken away from you, and will be given to a people who bear fruits.
MAT_21_44: And whoever falls on this stone will be broken, and on whomever it falls it will scatter him.
MAT_22_2: The kingdom of heaven is like a king who gave a marriage-feast for his son.
MAT_22_3: And he sent his servants to call those who were invited to the marriage-feast, but they would not come.
MAT_22_4: Again he sent other servants and said, Tell those who are invited, Behold my supper is ready, and my oxen and fatlings are killed, and everything is prepared; come to the marriage-feast.
MAT_22_5: But they sneered at it, and went away, one to his field, another to his business;
MAT_22_6: And the rest seized his servants and insulted them, and killed them.
MAT_22_7: When the king heard it he was angry; and he sent out his armies and destroyed those murderers, and burned their city.
MAT_22_8: Then he said to his servants, Now the marriage-feast is ready, and those who were invited were unworthy.
MAT_22_9: Go, therefore, to the main roads, and whomever you may find, invite them to the marriage- feast.
MAT_22_10: So the servants went out to the roads and gathered together every one they could find, bad and good; and the wedding-house was filled with guests.
MAT_22_11: When the king entered to see the guests, he saw there a man who was not wearing wedding garments.
MAT_22_12: And he said to him, My friend, how did you enter here, when you do not have wedding garments? And he was speechless.
MAT_22_13: Then the king said to the servants, Bind his hands and his feet and take him out into darkness; there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
MAT_22_14: For many are invited, and few are chosen.
MAT_22_18: But Jesus knew their evil, and said, Why do you tempt me, O hypocrites?
MAT_22_19: Show me the head-tax penny. And they brought to him a penny.
MAT_22_20: And Jesus said to them, Whose is this image and inscription?
MAT_22_21: They said, Caesar’s. He said to them, Give therefore to Caesar what is Caesar’s, and to God what is God’s.
MAT_22_29: Jesus answered, and said to them, You err, because you do not understand the scriptures nor the power of God.
MAT_22_30: For at the resurrection of the dead, they neither marry women, nor are women given to men in marriage, but they are like the angels of God in heaven.
MAT_22_31: But concerning the resurrection of the dead, have you not read what was said to you by God, saying,
MAT_22_32: I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, the God of Jacob? And yet God is not the God of the dead, but of the living.
MAT_22_37: Jesus said to him, Love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your power, and with all your mind.
MAT_22_38: This is the greatest and the first commandment.
MAT_22_39: And the second is like to it, Love your neighbor as yourself.
MAT_22_40: On these two commandments hang the law and the prophets.
MAT_22_42: And he said, What do you say concerning the Christ? whose son is he? They said to him, son of David.
MAT_22_43: He said to them, How is it then that David through the Spirit calls him Lord? For he said,
MAT_22_44: The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right hand, until I put your enemies under your feet.
MAT_22_45: If David then calls him Lord, how can he be his son?
MAT_23_2: And he said to them, The scribes and the Pharisees sit on the chair of Moses.
MAT_23_3: Therefore whatever they tell you to obey, obey and do it, but do not do according to their works; for they say and do not.
MAT_23_4: And they bind heavy burdens, and put them on men’s shoulders, but they themselves are not willing to touch them, even with their finger.
MAT_23_5: And all their works they do, just to be seen by men; for they widen the fringes of their garments, and they lengthen the ends of their robes,
MAT_23_6: And they like the chief places at feasts, and the front seats in the synagogues,
MAT_23_7: And the greetings in the streets, and to be called by men, Rabbi.
MAT_23_8: But you do not be called, Rabbi; for one is your Master, and all you are brethren.
MAT_23_9: And call no one on earth, father, for one is your Father in heaven.
MAT_23_10: Nor be called leaders, for one is your leader, the Christ.
MAT_23_11: But he who is greatest among you, let him be your minister.
MAT_23_12: For whoever exalts himself shall be humbled; and whoever humbles himself shall be exalted.
MAT_23_13: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for you embezzle the property of widows, with the pretense that you make long prayers; because of this you shall receive a greater judgment.*
MAT_23_15: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for you traverse sea and land to make one proselyte; and when he becomes one, you make him the son of hell twice more than yourselves.
MAT_23_16: Woe to you, blind guides, for you say, Whoever swears by the temple, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gold which is in the temple, he is guilty!
MAT_23_17: O you fools and blind! for which is greater, the gold or the temple that sanctifies the gold?
MAT_23_18: And whoever swears by the altar, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the offering that is on it, he is guilty.
MAT_23_19: O you fools and blind! for which is greater, the offering, or the altar that sanctifies the offering?
MAT_23_20: Therefore he who swears by the altar, he swears by it and by everything that is on it.
MAT_23_21: And whoever swears by the temple, swears by it and by him who dwells in it.
MAT_23_22: And he who swears by heaven, swears by the throne of God, and by him who sits on it.
MAT_23_23: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for you take tithes on mint, dill, and cummin, and you have overlooked the more important matters of the law, such as justice, mercy, and trustworthiness. These were necessary for you to have done, and the same by no means to have left undone.
MAT_23_24: O blind guides, who strain at gnats and swallow camels!
MAT_23_25: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! you clean the outside of the cup and of the dish, but inside they are full of extortion and iniquity.
MAT_23_26: Blind Pharisees! clean first the inside of the cup and of the dish, so that their outside may also be clean.
MAT_23_27: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for you are like tombs painted white, which look beautiful from the outside, but inside are full of dead bones and all kinds of corruption.
MAT_23_28: Even so, from the outside you appear to men to be righteous, but from within you are full of iniquity and hypocrisy.
MAT_23_29: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for you build the tombs of the prophets, and you decorate the graves of the righteous;
MAT_23_30: And you say, If we had been living in the days of our forefathers, we would not have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets.
MAT_23_31: Now you testify concerning yourselves, that you are the children of those who killed the prophets.
MAT_23_32: You also fill up the measure of your fathers.
MAT_23_33: O you serpents, and seed of scorpions! how can you flee from the judgment of hell?
MAT_23_34: Because of this, I am sending to you prophets and wise men and scribes; some of them you will kill and crucify; and some you will scourge in your synagogues, and pursue them from city to city;
MAT_23_35: So that all the blood of the righteous shed on the ground may come on you, from the blood of Abel the righteous down to the blood of Zachariah, son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the temple and the altar.
MAT_23_36: Truly I say to you, All of these things shall come upon this generation.
MAT_23_37: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, murderess of the prophets, and stoner of those who are sent to her! how often I wanted to gather together your children, just as a hen gathers her chickens under her wings, and yet you would not!
MAT_23_38: Behold, your house will be left to you desolate.
MAT_23_39: For I say to you, from now you will not see me until you say, Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord.
MAT_24_2: But he said to them, Behold, do you not see all of these? truly I say to you, Not a stone shall be left here upon a stone, which will not be torn down.
MAT_24_4: Jesus answered and said to them, Be careful that no man deceives you.
MAT_24_5: For many will come in my name, and say, I am the Christ, and they will deceive many.
MAT_24_6: You are bound to hear of revolutions and rumors of wars; look out and do not be disturbed; for all of these things must come to pass, but the end is not yet.
MAT_24_7: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom; and there will be famines and plagues and earthquakes, in different places.
MAT_24_8: But all these things are just the beginning of travail.
MAT_24_9: Then they will deliver you over to be oppressed, and they will kill you; and you will be hated by all nations for my name’s sake.
MAT_24_10: Then many will stumble, and they will hate one another, and betray one another.
MAT_24_11: And many false prophets will rise, and will mislead a great many.
MAT_24_12: And because of the growth of iniquity, the love of many will become cold.
MAT_24_13: But he who has patience to the end, he will be saved.
MAT_24_14: And this gospel of the kingdom shall be preached throughout the world as a testimony to all the nations; then the end will come.
MAT_24_15: When you see the sign of the refuse of desolation, as spoken by the prophet Daniel, accumulating in the holy place, whoever reads will understand it.
MAT_24_16: Then let those who are in Judaea, flee to the mountain,
MAT_24_17: And he who is on the roof, let him not come down to take things out of his house.
MAT_24_18: And he who is in the field, let him not return back to take his clothes.
MAT_24_19: But woe to those who are with child, and to those who give suck in those days!
MAT_24_20: Pray that your flight may not be in winter, nor on the sabbath.
MAT_24_21: For then will be great suffering, such as has never happened from the beginning of the world until now, and never will be again.
MAT_24_22: And if those days were not shortened, no flesh would live; but for the sake of the chosen ones those days will be shortened.
MAT_24_23: Then if any man should say to you, Behold, here is the Christ, or there, do not believe it.
MAT_24_24: For there will rise false Christs and lying prophets, and they will show signs and great wonders, so as to mislead, if possible, even the chosen ones.
MAT_24_25: Behold, I have told you in advance.
MAT_24_26: Therefore, if they should say to you, Behold, he is in the desert, do not go out; or, behold, he is in the room, do not believe it.
MAT_24_27: For just as the lightning comes out from the east, and is seen even in the west, so will be the coming of the Son of man.
MAT_24_28: For wherever the corpse is, there will the eagles gather.
MAT_24_29: Immediately after the suffering of those days, the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give her light, and the stars will fall from the sky, and the powers of the universe will be shaken.
MAT_24_30: Then the sign of the Son of man will appear in the sky; and then all the generations of the earth will mourn, and they will see the Son of man coming on the clouds of the sky, with an army and great glory.
MAT_24_31: And he will send his angels with a large trumpet, and they will gather his chosen ones from the four winds, from one end of the universe to the other.
MAT_24_32: From the fig tree learn a parable. As soon as its branches become tender and bring forth leaves, you know that summer is coming.
MAT_24_33: So even you, when you see all these things, know that it has arrived at the door.
MAT_24_34: Truly I say to you, that this generation will not pass away, until all these things happen.
MAT_24_35: Even heaven and earth will pass away, but my words shall not pass away.
MAT_24_36: But concerning that day and that hour, no man knows, not even the angels of heaven, but the Father alone.
MAT_24_37: Just as in the days of Noah, so will be the coming of the Son of man.
MAT_24_38: For as the people before the flood were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day Noah entered into the ark,
MAT_24_39: And they knew nothing until the flood came and carried them all away; such will be the coming of the Son of man.
MAT_24_40: Then two men will be in the field, one will be taken away and the other left.
MAT_24_41: Two women will be grinding at the handmill, one will be taken and the other left.
MAT_24_42: Be alert, therefore, for you do not know at what hour your Lord will come.
MAT_24_43: But know this much, that if the master of the house knew at what watch of the night the thief comes, he would keep awake and would not let his house be plundered.
MAT_24_44: For this reason, you also be ready, for the Son of man will come at an hour when you do not expect him.
MAT_24_45: Who then is the faithful and wise servant, whom his Lord has appointed over his household, to give them food in due time?
MAT_24_46: Blessed is that servant, when his Lord comes and finds him so doing.
MAT_24_47: Truly I say to you, he will appoint him over all that he has.
MAT_24_48: But if a bad servant should say in his heart, My lord will delay his coming,
MAT_24_49: And he begins to beat his fellow servants, and to eat and drink with drunkards,
MAT_24_50: The Lord of that servant will come on a day when he does not expect, and at an hour that he does not know.
MAT_24_51: And he will severely scourge him, and give him a portion like that of the hypocrites; there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
MAT_25_1: THEN the kingdom of heaven will be like ten virgins, who took their lamps, and went out to greet the bridegroom and the bride.
MAT_25_2: Five of them were wise, and five were foolish.
MAT_25_3: And the foolish ones took their lamps, but took no oil with them.
MAT_25_4: But the wise ones took oil in the vessels with their lamps.
MAT_25_5: As the bridegroom was delayed, they all slumbered and slept.
MAT_25_6: And at midnight there was a cry, Behold, the bridegroom is coming; go out to greet him.
MAT_25_7: Then all the virgins got up and fixed their lamps.
MAT_25_8: And the foolish ones said to the wise ones, Give us some of your oil, for our lamps are going out.
MAT_25_9: Then the wise ones answered and said, Why, there would not be enough for us and for you; go to those who sell and buy for yourselves.
MAT_25_10: And while they went to buy, the bridegroom came; and those who were ready entered with him into the wedding house, and the door was locked.
MAT_25_11: Afterward the other virgins also came and said, Our lord, our lord, open to us.
MAT_25_12: But he answered and said to them, Truly I say to you, I do not know you.
MAT_25_13: Be alert, therefore, for you do not know, that day nor the hour.
MAT_25_14: It is just like a man who went on a journey, who called his servants and put his wealth in their charge.
MAT_25_15: To one he gave five talents, to one two, to another one; to each one according to his ability; and immediately he went on a journey.
MAT_25_16: The one who received five talents then went and traded with them, and he earned five others.
MAT_25_17: Likewise the second one, he gained by trading gained two others.
MAT_25_18: But he who received one, went and dug in the ground, and hid his lord’s money.
MAT_25_19: After a long time, the lord of those servants returned, and took an accounting from them.
MAT_25_20: Then the one who received five talents came up, and offered five others, and he said, My lord, you gave me five talents; behold, I have gained five others to them.
MAT_25_21: His lord said to him, Well done, good and reliable servant; you have been faithful over a little, I will appoint you over much; enter into your master’s joy.
MAT_25_22: Then the one with the two talents came and he said, My lord, you gave me two talents, behold I have gained two others to them.
MAT_25_23: His lord said to him, Well done, good and reliable servant, you have been faithful over a little, I will appoint you over much; enter into your master’s joy.
MAT_25_24: Then the one who received one talent also came up, and he said, My lord, I knew that you are a hard man, and you reap where you did not sow, and gather where you did not scatter.
MAT_25_25: So I was afraid, and I went and hid your talent in the ground; here it is, it is your own one.
MAT_25_26: His lord answered and said to him, O wicked and lazy servant, you knew me that I reap where I did not sow, and I gather where I did not scatter.
MAT_25_27: You should then have put my money in the exchange, and when I returned I would have demanded my own with interest.
MAT_25_28: Therefore take away the talent from him, and give it to the one who has ten talents.
MAT_25_29: For to him who has, it shall he given, and it shall increase to him; but he who has not, even that which he has shall be taken away from him.
MAT_25_30: And the idle servant they threw into the outer darkness; there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
MAT_25_31: When the Son of man comes in his glory, and all his holy angels with him, then he will sit upon the throne of his glory.
MAT_25_32: And all nations will gather before him; and he will separate them one from another, just as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats;
MAT_25_33: And he will set the sheep at his right, and the goats at his left.
MAT_25_34: Then the King will say to those at his right, Come, you blessed of my Father, inherit the kingdom which has been prepared for you from the foundation of the world.
MAT_25_35: For I was hungry, and you gave me to eat; I was thirsty, and you gave me to drink; I was a stranger and you took me in;
MAT_25_36: I was naked, and you covered me; I was sick, and you visited me; I was in prison, and you came to me.
MAT_25_37: Then the righteous will say to him, Our Lord, when did we see you hungry, and feed you? or thirsty and gave you drink?
MAT_25_38: And when did we see you a stranger, and took you in? Or that you were naked, and covered you?
MAT_25_39: And when did we see you sick, or in the prison, and come to you?
MAT_25_40: The king then will answer and say to them, Truly I tell you, Inasmuch as you have done it to one of these least brethren, you did it to me.
MAT_25_41: Then he will also say to those at his left, Go away from me, you cursed, to the everlasting fire, which is prepared for the adversary and his angels.
MAT_25_42: For I was hungry, and you did not give me to eat; I was thirsty, and you did not give me to drink;
MAT_25_43: I was a stranger, and you did not take me in; I was naked, and you did not cover me; I was sick and in prison and you did not visit me.
MAT_25_44: Then they also will answer and say, Our lord, when did we see you hungry, or thirsty, or a stranger, or naked, or sick or in the prison, and did not minister to you?
MAT_25_45: Then he will answer and say to them, Truly I say to you, Inasmuch as you did not do it to one of these least ones, you also did not do it to me.
MAT_25_46: And these shall go into everlasting torment, and the righteous into eternal life.

LUK_12_1: WHEN a large number of people had gathered together so that they trod on one another, Jesus began to say to his disciples first of all, Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.

LUK_16_1: AND he spoke a parable to his disciples, saying, There was a rich man, who had a steward; and they accused him that he was wasting his wealth.

JHN_3_8: The wind blows where it pleases, and you hear its sound; but you do not know whence it comes and whither it goes; such is every man who is born of the Spirit.
JHN_3_10: Jesus answered, You are a teacher of Israel, and yet you do not understand these things?
JHN_3_11: Truly, truly, I say to you, We speak only what we know, and we testify only to what we have seen; and yet you do not accept our testimony.
JHN_3_12: If I have told you about earthly things and you do not believe, how then will you believe me if I tell you about heavenly things?
JHN_3_13: No man has ascended to heaven except him who came down from heaven, even the Son of man who is in heaven.
JHN_3_14: Just as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, so the Son of man is ready to be lifted up;
JHN_3_15: So that every man who believes in him should not perish, but have eternal life.
JHN_3_16: For God so loved the world that he even gave his only begotten Son, so that whoever believes in him should not perish, but have eternal life.
JHN_3_17: For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world; but that the world should be saved by him.
JHN_3_18: He who believes in him will not be condemned; and he who does not believe has already been condemned for not believing in the name of the only begotten Son of God.
JHN_3_19: And this is the judgment, that light has come into the world and yet men have loved darkness more than light, because their works were evil.
JHN_3_20: For every one who does detestable things hates the light, and he does not come to the light, because his works cannot be covered.
JHN_3_21: But he who does truthful things comes to the light, so that his works may be known, that they are done through God.
JHN_4_7: And there came a woman from Samaria to draw water; and Jesus said to her, Give me water to drink.
JHN_4_10: Jesus answered, saying to her, If you only knew the gift of God and who is the man who said to you, Give me a drink, you would have asked him, and he would pave given you living water.
JHN_4_13: Jesus answered, saying to her, Everyone who drinks of this water will thirst again;
JHN_4_14: But whoever drinks of the water which I give him shall never thirst; but the same water which I give him shall become in him a well of water springing up to life everlasting·
JHN_4_16: Jesus said to her, Go and call your husband, and come here.
JHN_4_17: She said to him, I have no husband. Jesus said to her, You said well, I have no husband;
JHN_4_18: For you have had five husbands; and the one you now have is not your husband; what you said is true.
JHN_4_21: Jesus said to her, Woman, believe me, the time is coming, when neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem will they worship the Father.
JHN_4_22: You worship what you do not know; but we worship what we do know; for salvation is from the Jews.
JHN_4_23: But the time is coming, and it is here, when the true worshippers shall worship the Father in spirit and in truth; for the Father also desires worshippers such as these.

JHN_4_26: Jesus said to her, I am he who is speaking to you.
JHN_4_32: But he said to them, I have food to eat of which you do not know.

JHN_4_35: Do you not say that after four months comes the harvest? Behold, I say to you, Lift up your eyes and look at the fields which have turned white and have long been ready for the harvest.
JHN_4_36: And he who reaps receives wages and gathers fruits to life everlasting, so that the sower and the reaper may rejoice together.
JHN_4_37: For in this case the saying is true, One sows and another reaps.

JHN_5_20: For the Father loves his Son, and he shows him everything that he does; and he will show him greater works than these, so that you may marvel.

JHN_5_22: For the Father does not judge any man, but he has entrusted all judgment to the Son;

JHN_5_24: Truly, truly, I say to you, He who hears my word and believes him who has sent me has everlasting life; and he does not come before the judgment, but he passes from death to life.
JHN_5_25: Truly, truly, I say to you, The time is coming, and it is now already here, when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God; and those who hear it will live.
JHN_5_26: For as the Father has life in himself, even so he has given to the Son also to have life in himself.
JHN_5_27: And he has given him authority to execute judgment also, for he is the Son of man.
JHN_5_28: Do not wonder at this; for the time is coming, when all those who are in their graves will hear his voice,
JHN_5_29: And they will come out; those who have done good works to the resurrection of life; and those who have done evil works to the resurrection of judgment.
JHN_5_30: I can do nothing of myself; but as I hear I judge, and my judgment is just; for I do not seek my own will, but the will of him who sent me.
JHN_5_31: If I testify concerning myself, my testimony is not true.
JHN_5_32: It is another one who testifies concerning me; and I know that the testimony which he testifies concerning me is true.
JHN_5_33: You sent to John, and he testified concerning the truth.
JHN_5_34: But I do not receive any testimony from men; but I tell you these things so that you may be saved.
JHN_5_35: He was a lamp which burns and gives light; and you were willing to delight in his light for a while.
JHN_5_36: But I have a greater testimony than that of John; for the works which my Father has given me to finish, the same works which I do, testify concerning me, that the Father has sent me.
JHN_5_37: And the Father who sent me has testified concerning me. But you have never heard his voice nor seen his appearance.
JHN_5_38: And his word does not abide in you, because you do not believe in him whom he has sent.
JHN_5_39: Examine the scriptures; in them you trust that you have eternal life; and even they testify concerning me.
JHN_5_40: Yet you will not come to me, that you might have life everlasting.
JHN_5_41: I do not receive any praise from men.
JHN_5_42: But I know you well, that the love of God is not in you.
JHN_5_43: I have come in the name of my Father, and you do not receive me; if another should come in his own name, you will receive him.
JHN_5_44: How can you believe, when you accept praise one from another, but the praise from God only, you do not want?
JHN_5_45: What! do you think that I will accuse you before the Father; there is one who will accuse you, even Moses, in whom you trust.
JHN_5_46: For if you had believed in Moses, you would also have believed in me; for Moses wrote concerning me.
JHN_5_47: If you do not believe his writings, how then can you believe my words?
JHN_6_5: And Jesus lifted up his eyes and saw a large crowd coming to him, and he said to Philip, Where can we buy bread that all these may eat?

JHN_6_20: But Jesus said to them, It is I, do not be afraid.
JHN_6_26: Jesus answered, saying to them, Truly, truly, I say to you, You seek me, not because you saw the miracles, but just because you ate bread and were filled.
JHN_6_27: Do not labor for the food which perishes, but for the food which endures to life everlasting, which the Son of man will give you; for this one God the Father has sealed.
JHN_6_29: Jesus answered, saying to them, This is the work of God, that you should believe in him whom he has sent.
JHN_6_32: Jesus said to them, Truly, truly, I say to you, It was not Moses who gave you bread from heaven; but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven.
JHN_6_33: For the bread of God is he who has come down from heaven, who gives life to the world.

JHN_6_39: This is the will of him who sent me that I should lose nothing of all that he has given me, but should raise it up at the last day.
JHN_6_40: For this is the will of my Father, that whoever sees the Son and believes in him shall have life everlasting; and I will raise him up at the last day.
JHN_6_41: Now the Jews murmured against him, for he said, I am the bread which came down from heaven.

JHN_6_50: This is the bread which came down from heaven, that a man may eat of it and not die.
JHN_6_51: I am the living bread because I came down from heaven; if any man eats of this bread, he shall live forever; and the bread which I will give is my body, which I am giving for the sake of the life of the world.
JHN_6_53: Jesus said to them. Truly, truly I say to you, Unless you eat the body of the Son of man and drink his blood, you have no life in yourselves,
JHN_6_54: He who eats of my body and drinks of my blood has eternal life; and I will raise him at the last day.
JHN_6_55: For my body truly is the food, and my blood truly is the drink.
JHN_6_56: He who eats my body and drinks my blood will abide with me, and I with him.
JHN_6_57: Just as the living Father sent me, and I am living because of the Father, so whoever eats of me will also live because of me.
JHN_6_58: This is the bread which came down from heaven; it is not like that manna which your forefathers ate and died; he who eats of this bread shall live forever.
JHN_6_61: Jesus knew in himself that his disciples were murmuring about this; so he said to them, Does this cause you to stumble?
JHN_6_62: What then if you should see the Son of man ascending to the place where he was before?
JHN_6_63: It is the spirit that gives life; the body is of no account; the words which I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
JHN_6_64: But there are some of you who do not believe. For Jesus knew for a long while who were those who did not believe, and who was to betray him.
JHN_6_65: And he said to them, For this reason I have told you that no man can come to me unless it is given to him by my Father.

JHN_7_17: He who wills to do his will can understand if my teaching is from God or if I am just speaking of my own accord.
JHN_7_18: He who speaks of his own accord seeks glory for himself; but he who seeks the glory of him who sent him is true, and there is no deception in his heart.
JHN_7_19: Did not Moses give you the law? And yet no one of you obeys the law. Why do you want to kill me?
JHN_7_21: Jesus answered, saying to them, I have done one work, and all of you marvel.
JHN_7_22: Moses gave you circumcision, not because it is from Moses, but because it is from the forefathers; and yet you circumcise a man on the sabbath.
JHN_7_23: So if a man is circumcised on the sabbath day, that the law of Moses may not be broken; yet you murmur at me, because I healed a whole man on the sabbath day?
JHN_7_24: Do not judge by partiality, but judge a just judgment.
JHN_7_28: Jesus then lifted up his voice as he taught in the temple, saying, You know me, and you know whence I come; and yet I have not come of my own accord, but he who sent me is true, whom you do not know.
JHN_7_29: But I know him; because I am from him, and he sent me.

JHN_7_34: You will seek me, but you will not find me; and where I am you cannot come.
JHN_7_36: What does this word mean which he said, You will seek me and you will not find me, and where I am you cannot come?
JHN_7_37: Now on the greatest day, which is the last day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried out, saying, If any man is thirsty, let him come to me and drink.
JHN_7_38: Whoever believes in me, just as the scriptures have said, the rivers of living water shall flow from within him.
JHN_8_7: When they were through questioning him, he straightened himself up and said to them, He who is among you without sin, let him first throw a stone at her.
JHN_8_10: When Jesus straightened himself up, he said to the woman, Where are they? Did no man condemn you?
JHN_8_11: She said, No man, Lord. Then Jesus said, Neither do I condemn you; go away, and from henceforth, do not sin again.
JHN_8_12: Again Jesus spoke to them, saying, I am the light of the world; he who follows me shall not walk in darkness, but he shall find for himself the light of life.
JHN_8_14: Jesus answered, saying to them, Even though I testify concerning myself, my testimony is true, because I know whence I came and whither I go; but you do not know whence I came or whither I go.
JHN_8_15: You judge according to the flesh; but I judge no man.
JHN_8_16: And if I should judge, my judgment is true; because I am not doing it alone, but I and my Father who sent me.
JHN_8_17: And it is written in your own law that the testimony of two men is true.
JHN_8_18: I testify concerning myself, and my Father who sent me testifies concerning me.
JHN_8_19: They said to him, Where is your Father? Jesus answered, saying to them, You know neither me nor my Father; if you knew me, you would know my Father also.
JHN_8_21: Jesus again said to them, I am going away, and you will seek me, and you will die in your sins; and where I am going you cannot come.
JHN_8_22: The Jews said, What! will he kill himself? For he says, Where I am going, you cannot come.
JHN_8_23: And he said to them, You are from below, and I am from above; you are of this world, but I am not of this world.
JHN_8_24: I told you that you will die in your sins; for unless you believe that I am he, you will die in your sins.
JHN_8_25: The Jews said, Who are you? Jesus said to them, Even though I should begin to speak to you,

JHN_8_28: Again Jesus said to them, When you have lifted up the Son of man, then you will understand that I am he, and I do nothing of my own accord; but as my Father has taught me, so I speak just like him.
JHN_8_29: And he who sent me is with me; and my Father has never left me alone, because I always do what pleases him.
JHN_8_31: Then Jesus said to the Jews who believed in him, If you abide by my word, you are truly my disciples.
JHN_8_32: And you will know the truth, and that very truth will make you free.
JHN_8_34: Jesus said to them, Truly, truly, I say to you, Whoever commits sin is a servant of sin.
JHN_8_35: And a servant does not remain in the house forever, but the son remains forever.
JHN_8_36: If therefore, the Son shall make you free, you shall be free indeed.
JHN_8_37: I know you are the descendants of Abraham; but still you want to kill me, because you have no room in you for my word.
JHN_8_38: I speak what I have seen with my Father; and you do what you have seen with your father.
JHN_8_39: They answered, saying to him, Our own father is Abraham. Jesus said to them, If you were the sons of Abraham, you would be doing the works of Abraham.
JHN_8_40: But behold, now you want to kill me, even a man who has told you the truth which I heard from God; this Abraham did not do.
JHN_8_41: But you do the works of your father. They said to him, We are not born of fornication; we have one Father, God.
JHN_8_42: Jesus said to them, If God were your Father, you would love me, for I proceeded and came from God; I did not come of my own accord, but he sent me.
JHN_8_43: Why therefore do you not understand my word? Because you cannot obey my word?
JHN_8_44: You are from the father of accusation, and you want to do the lusts of your father, he who is a murderer of men from the very beginning and who never stands by the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he speaks he speaks his own lie, because he is a liar, and the father of lies.
JHN_8_45: But because I speak the truth, you do not believe me.
JHN_8_46: Which one of you can rebuke me because of sin? If I speak the truth, why do you not believe me?
JHN_8_47: He who is of God, hears God’s words; for this reason you do not hear, because you are not of God.
JHN_8_49: Jesus said to them, I am not crazy; but I honor my Father, and you curse me.
JHN_8_50: I do not seek my glory; there is one who seeks and judges.
JHN_8_51: Truly, truly, I say to you, Whoever obeys my word shall never see death.
JHN_8_54: Jesus said to them, If I honor myself, my honor is nothing; but it is my Father who honors me, the one of whom you say, He is our God.
JHN_8_55: Yet you have not known him, but I know him; and if I should say, I do not know him, I would be a liar like yourselves; but I do know him, and I obey his word.
JHN_8_56: Your father Abraham rejoiced to see my day; and he saw it and was glad.
JHN_8_58: Jesus said to them, Truly, truly, I say to you, Before Abraham was born, I was.

JHN_9_5: As long as I am in the world, I am the light of the world.

JHN_10_1: TRULY, truly, I say to you, He who does not enter by the door into the sheepfold, but climbs up from another place, is a thief and a bandit.
JHN_10_2: But he who enters by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.
JHN_10_3: To him the doorkeeper opens the door, and the sheep hear his voice, and he calls his own sheep by their names and brings them out.
JHN_10_4: And when he has brought out his sheep, he goes before them; and his own sheep follow him, because they know his voice.
JHN_10_5: The sheep do not follow a stranger, but they run away from him, because they do not know the voice of a stranger.
JHN_10_7: Jesus said to them again, Truly, truly, I say to you, I am the door of the sheep.
JHN_10_8: All who have come are thieves and bandits, if the sheep did not hear them.
JHN_10_9: I am the door; if any man enter by me, he shall live and he shall come in and go out and find pasture.
JHN_10_10: A thief does not come, except to steal and kill and destroy; I have come that they might have life, and have it abundantly.
JHN_10_11: I am the good shepherd; a good shepherd risks his life for the sake of his sheep.
JHN_10_12: But the hired person who is not the shepherd and who is not the owner of the sheep, when he sees the wolf coming, leaves the sheep and runs away; and the wolf comes and seizes and scatters the sheep.
JHN_10_13: The hired person runs away because he is hired and does not care for the sheep.
JHN_10_14: I am the good shepherd, and I know my own, and my own know me.
JHN_10_15: Just as my Father knows me, I also know my Father; and I lay down my life for the sake of the sheep.
JHN_10_16: I have other sheep also, which are not of this fold; them too I must bring, and they will hear my voice; and all the sheep will become one flock and one shepherd.
JHN_10_17: This is why my Father loves me, because I lay down my life so that I may take it up again.
JHN_10_18: No man takes it away from me, but I lay it down of my own will. Therefore I have the power to lay it down, and I have the power to take it up again. This command I received from my Father.
JHN_10_25: Jesus answered, saying to them, I have told you, but you do not believe; yet the works which I do in the name of my Father testify of me.
JHN_10_26: But you do not believe, because you are not of my sheep, just as I told you.
JHN_10_27: My own sheep hear my voice, and I know them and they follow me;
JHN_10_28: And I give to them eternal life; and they will never perish, and no man will snatch them from my hands.
JHN_10_29: For my Father who gave them to me is greater than all; and no man can snatch anything from my Father’s hand.
JHN_10_30: I and my Father are of one accord.
JHN_10_32: Jesus said to them, I have shown you many good works from my Father; for which one of them do you stone me?
JHN_10_34: Jesus said to them, Is it not so written in your law, I said, you are gods?
JHN_10_35: If he called them gods because the word of God was with them (and the scripture cannot be broken)
JHN_10_36: Why to the one whom the Father sanctified and sent to the world, do you say, You blaspheme, just because I said to you, I am the Son of God.
JHN_10_37: If I am not doing the works of my Father, do not believe me.
JHN_10_38: But if I am doing them, even though you do not believe in me, believe in the works, so that you may know and believe that my Father is with me and I am with my Father.
